trigger_keywords: vino tinto de la docg barolo

steps.label: cause
steps.text: Nebbiolo-based Barolo begins with high tannin and high acidity, providing substantial structure for ageing.

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: During bottle ageing tannins polymerise and soften while primary aromas evolve gradually into more developed tertiary compounds.

steps.label: effect
steps.text: Mature Barolo retains acidity and structure while developing tertiary notes such as dried flowers, leather, earth, and tar.

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Formative causal formulation: Mature Barolo retains acidity and structure while developing tertiary notes such as dried flowers, leather, earth, and tar.

_meta.source_note: Inferred formative model - not official WSET assessment or mark-scheme content
